item.title: Snoop Dogg Already IN with the Toddler Generation - Michael Bublé's 6-Year Kid is a Fan.
item.description:
Snoop Dogg has a kid's album Doggyland, which he made for his own grandchildren. In addition to "Affirmations," it features hit nursery rhyme-style tunes like "Please & Thank You," "Sharing Is Caring," "Everybody's Different," "Wheels on the Bus," and more, all with a hip-hop twist.
https://parade.com/news/fans-say-snoop-dogg-most-wholesome-michael-buble-shares-video-meeting-daughter-vida
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

item.title: This is my final bottom shelf post
item.description:
This will be my final post on Imgur until the Extra-Life 2024 finale in November. Beginning tonight at midnight we will start our 744 hour Extra-Life streamathon. Once I hit "Go live" tonight at midnight, I will not hit "End Stream" until midnight of November 1st.
but in the meantime, here's #adayinthelife of a bottom shelf memes shitposter
@HileyNoteson Yoooooooooooooooooooooo
We will be panhandling for Extra-Life for a 744 hour NON-STOP streamaton on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka starting TONIGHT. Am I stupid? Yes. But am I insane? Also yes. What's a streamathon you ask? Well as @minoshirokin put it: a streamathon is the internet version of a telethon for charity lasting all of October. We'll be back to posting memes when it is over in November.
Milestones above/Incentives below.
We are still reaching out to any artists who would like to participate in the auction. For those who are interested, the deadline to post is October 19th on discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka Keep in mind that Imgur's Secret Santa is right around the corner! You can knock out three birds with one stone by supporting our Imgur community artists, Children's Hospital, AND finding the perfect gift for your future match. Imgur, by far, has the absolute most wholesome community when compared to any other shit-posting platforms. This time last year, you all donated $12,458 to the kids. Because of you, children can get the medical attention that they NEED, not just the medical attention their parents can afford.
If you pop into twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka at any moment during October, there is a 100% chance you will catch us live.
Why yes. Yes they are. Extra-life.org/participant/bottomshelfvodka (If you find yourself needing to scratch that memes itch, there are plenty posted 24/7 in the meme dumpster channel on discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka )
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: A day in the life of a Florida Teacher
item.description:
My school is one of the earlier starting middle schools but I live close. I get up at 6:30am and get breakfast before coming to my classroom. Then, I make sure all the laptops are plugged in, that there are enough pencils sharpened, and I put out the paper copies for the students who prefer to work by hand.
I write the date (twice) and then the board. The left is the useful side; supplies tell kids what to grab and out the gate is what they should begin with. The left is the government mandated side where I put up the standards we're learning, the goal of the lesson, the big question "How can I..." And then what I'll do to check for understanding.
This is my teacher station! I take attendance every class and set up slides on the board. This week, we're reading and practicing some narrative writing.
The board behind my area is where I put pictures kids draw for me. I bought headphones because some kids like to listen to music while they work and we're a non-cellphone school. Compromise! I now have my laptop card in the wall crook but it's not there yet.
This is the student center when students come in, they grab their laptop/any papers they need and sometimes noise cancelling headphones if they want them. Then, they follow the arrows to their seat.
Said arrows. I learned about not managing traffic flow last year the hard way. From this angle on the board you can see the desk where kids sign out for the bathroom. I have 6 classes a day; 2 gen ed, 1 high needs, 3 honors. My honors classes are all at the end of the day which is really nice.
A lot of teachers don't like the rolly desks. I love them. They're super comfy AND it's way easier for kids to get into groups or get away from their groups. Sometimes kids spin in place and work. I don't mind that at all. They're working! My area of Florida recently added this test we have to do in ELA every three weeks that's only supposed to take 25 minutes. That's a full lie. Every three weeks I lose a class for a reading test. Our schools has a reading class that's mandatory so the kids complain a LOT because I'm mysteriously doing a reading test for our mostly writing focused class.
The classroom before. The things on the table are mine. School ends but there isn't really time to complete grading at school. My planning is 2nd period and my lunch is 4a. I have 4 classes after that and 147 students so after students leave, I pack up and do my work at home. I am grateful that my boss lets me leave whenever so long as my work gets done. If I have tests, that's usually just an extra 2-3 hours. If it's essays, that's 10-20 minutes per student. This year, I'm focusing a lot more of teaching the kids how to edit and critique each other's essays so I only have to review essays twice (one draft, and then the final) instead of reviewing their first draft, doing their revised draft and then grading their final draft.
item.dc:creator: AranaDiscoteca

item.title: A rare phenomenon captured by a french pilot..
item.description: The "giant jet" is a rare, powerful lightning discharge that occurs from the top of a thunderstorm cloud, extending upward into the ionosphere, much higher than typical lightning. It connects the cloud to space, reaching altitudes of 70-90 km.Here an interesting video of (TLEs) phenomenons https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=tGPQ5kzJ9Tg
item.dc:creator: SomebodythatIusedtonope
